title: fraxinus crispa
intro: Fraxinus crispa, also known as the cut-leaf ash, is a deciduous tree native to China and parts of the Himalayas, characterized by its distinctive cut-leaf or pinnate foliage. Traditionally, the bark and leaves of Fraxinus crispa have been used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic compounds. The health benefits of Fraxinus crispa include its potential to aid in reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. Fraxinus crispa can be prepared into herbal teas, tinctures, or infusions for medicinal use.
